description: Implement block-search pricing (findEnteringArc) using the reducedCost helper.
commit: feat: add block-search pricing for network simplex pivot
steps: Block size: `blockSize := int(math.Ceil(math.Sqrt(float64(s.arcCount))))`; clamp to at least 1.

Scan arcs in blocks starting at s.nextBlockStart, wrapping modulo s.arcCount. For each arc in the current block: skip if arcState==stateTree; skip if |arcCost[a]| >= math.MaxInt64/4 (sentinel); compute rc := s.reducedCost(a); improving if (state==stateLower && rc < 0) or (state==stateUpper && rc > 0). Track the most-improving arc within the block (largest |rc| with the improving sign).

If the block yields an improver, update s.nextBlockStart to the position immediately after that block and return (bestArc, true). If the block has none, advance s.nextBlockStart by blockSize and continue; after scanning all arcCount arcs with no improver, return (0, false).

Create solver_pricing_test.go with `TestFindEnteringArc_Improving`: construct state literal (bypass initState) with arcCount=3, pi known, arcState all stateLower, and arc 1 configured so reducedCost(1) == -5 while others have reducedCost >= 0. Assert findEnteringArc returns (1, true).
Add `TestFindEnteringArc_NoImproving`: all reduced costs >= 0 for stateLower arcs. Assert (0, false).

Add `TestFindEnteringArc_SkipsSentinel`: arc 0 has arcCost = math.MaxInt64/3 (>= MaxInt64/4 threshold) AND a reducedCost that would otherwise be -1; other arcs non-improving. Assert (0, false) — sentinel skipped.
acceptance_criteria: Pricing uses the `reducedCost` helper from solver-reduced-cost, not an inlined formula.
